changed_functions.name: L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler
key_changes: CRITICAL: Added ProbeForRead(arg1, 0x10, 4) call when ExGetPreviousMode() is user-mode — unpatched version directly dereferences raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer at 0x1c0009c6f (movups xmm0, [rcx]) with NO ProbeForRead, NO ExGetPreviousMode check, and NO NULL check
Added NULL pointer check for arg1 (!arg1) before any dereference
Added ExGetPreviousMode() gate: only probes when previous mode is user-mode
Unpatched binary does NOT import ProbeForRead or ExGetPreviousMode at all; patched binary imports both
Moved the dereference of *arg1 to AFTER the size check (cmp edx, 0x10), NULL check, and probe
Struct offset shifts (0x84→0x8c, 0xd8→0xe0, 0x60→0x68, etc.) due to 8-byte struct layout change
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a missing ProbeForRead vulnerability (CWE-125). The handler is serviced via the Fast I/O device-control routine (FilterFastDeviceIoControl), which passes the caller's raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer without any I/O-manager copy or probe, even though the IOCTL is METHOD_BUFFERED (which would normally be copied under IRP dispatch). The unpatched code dereferences *arg1 (movups xmm0, xmmword [rcx] at 0x1c0009c6f) BEFORE any validation, allowing a user-mode caller to pass an invalid/fake pointer. Because the read happens before the InputBufferLength check (cmp edx, 0x10 at 0x1c0009c85), even a zero-length input triggers the dereference. The demonstrable impact is a reliable kernel denial of service (BSOD) by passing an unmapped pointer. The 16 bytes read are used only as two bridge-interface lookup keys and are never returned to the caller, so this is a blind read, not a self-contained information leak. The handler never writes to the user-supplied pointer.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (0x1c0009c5c):
mov     r11, rsp
mov     [r11+0x10], rbx
mov     [r11+0x18], rbp
push    rsi
push    rdi
push    r14
sub     rsp, 0x40
movups  xmm0, xmmword ptr [rcx]    ; <<< VULNERABLE: deref user ptr BEFORE any check
xor     eax, eax
xor     edi, edi
xor     esi, esi
mov     [r11+0x8], rax
xor     r14b, r14b
movdqu  xmmword ptr [rsp+0x30], xmm0
cmp     edx, 0x10               ; length check happens AFTER deref
jb      loc_1C0009D92
...

PATCHED (0x140011a28):
mov     [rsp+0x8], rsi
mov     [rsp+0x18], rdi
push    r12
push    r14
push    r15
sub     rsp, 0x60
mov     rdi, rcx                 ; save ptr, do NOT deref yet
xor     esi, esi
cmp     edx, 0x10               ; length check FIRST
jb      loc_140011B88
test    rcx, rcx                 ; <<< ADDED: NULL check
jz      loc_140011B88
test    r9d, r9d
jnz     loc_140011B88
test    r8, r8
jnz     loc_140011B88
call    cs:__imp_ExGetPreviousMode ; <<< ADDED: check caller mode
test    al, al
jz      short loc_140011A9B      ; skip probe if kernel-mode caller
lea     edx, [rsi+0x10]          ; Length = 0x10
lea     r8d, [rsi+0x4]           ; Alignment = 4
mov     rcx, rdi                 ; Address = arg1
call    cs:__imp_ProbeForRead    ; <<< ADDED: ProbeForRead(arg1, 0x10, 4)
movups  xmm0, xmmword ptr [rdi]  ; deref AFTER probe
movdqu  [rsp+0x48], xmm0
mov     rcx, qword ptr [rsp+0x48]
call    L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule

changed_functions.name: FilterFastDeviceIoControl
key_changes: Fast I/O dispatch handler for IOCTL 0x12a000 — passes raw user InputBuffer pointer (arg3/rcx) directly to L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler without any probing
Both versions pass the raw pointer; the fix is in the callee
Minor reordering of result store (mov [rcx],eax before mov [rcx+8],0)
changed_functions.security_notes: This is the attacker entry point. FilterFastDeviceIoControl is registered as the Fast I/O dispatch routine for the \Device\l2bridge device. When a user-mode process calls DeviceIoControl with IOCTL 0x12a000, the I/O manager invokes this Fast I/O path. Unlike IRP-based IOCTL dispatch with METHOD_BUFFERED, Fast I/O passes the raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer directly. The function passes arg3 (the raw InputBuffer) directly as arg1 to L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler, where it is dereferenced without probing in the unpatched version.

changed_functions.name: GetLinkLayerAddressFromND
key_changes: Changed ICMPv6 type validation from an arithmetic range check (unpatched 0x1c000260f: type + 0x79 <= 1, i.e. type 0x87 or 0x88) to explicit value comparisons (patched 0x14000241b: type == 0x87 || type == 0x88)
Changed code field validation from (byte - 1 <= 1, i.e. code 1 or 2) to explicit (code == 1 || code == 2)
Both forms accept exactly the same value sets; functionally equivalent refactor
changed_functions.security_notes: ICMPv6 Neighbor Discovery type/code validation rewritten from arithmetic range checks to explicit equality checks. The accepted value sets are identical ({0x87,0x88} for type, {1,2} for code), and both operands are single bytes, so the subtraction-based form admitted no additional value. This is a functionally equivalent refactor, not a security boundary change.

changed_functions.name: FilterRegisterDevice
key_changes: Device registered with characteristics 0x380185 and no security descriptor specified — relying on default NDIS device ACL
The symbolic link is \DosDevices\L2BRIDGE making it accessible as \\.\L2BRIDGE from user mode
Minor stack variable reordering between versions

changed_functions.name: L2BridgeCloneIPv6NBL
key_changes: Added LookupIPv6AddressInTable call in the else branch (non-multicast, non-ND) to look up destination MAC from IPv6 rewrite table
Struct offset changes (0x60→0x68, 0x64→0x6c) due to struct layout change
Removed post-clone byte-write at offset 0x15 that was in unpatched version
changed_functions.security_notes: Behavioral change in IPv6 packet cloning logic — adds rewrite-table lookup. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: ReceiveNetBufferList
key_changes: Restructured multicast/unicast decision logic — patched uses LookupIPv6AddressInTable and LookupMACAddressInTable with different call signatures
Struct offset shifts throughout (0x5c→0x64, 0x88→0x90, 0xa0→0xa8, 0xa1→0xa9, 0xa4→0xac, 0xd8→0xe0, etc.)
Rearranged control flow for multicast detection (0x33 multicast prefix check)
changed_functions.security_notes: Struct layout and call-signature changes. No new security checks added or removed.

changed_functions.name: SendNetBufferList
key_changes: Restructured multicast forwarding logic to match new function signatures
Added null-clone fallback (sends original NBL if clone fails)
Struct offset shifts throughout
changed_functions.security_notes: Behavioral restructuring matching new struct layout. No security boundary changes.

changed_functions.name: ForwardNBLToFilterModule
key_changes: Added HandleRSCPacket call and RSC (Receive Segment Coalescing) handling logic
Changed NBL context allocation size from 0x20 to 0x28
Struct offset shifts throughout
New var_30 and var_20 context fields
changed_functions.security_notes: New RSC feature integration. Context size increase and new packet handling function.

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry
key_changes: Added wil_InitializeFeatureStaging and feature gate infrastructure
Added TCP segmentation library initialization (IppBatchingLibContext, IppSegLibContext, SegLibInitialized)
Added NetioCopyNetBufferListQosInformation resolution via MmGetSystemRoutineAddress
Added SegLibHeap feature (Feature_TCPIP_2025_Wave2_SegLibHeap) and MdpCreatePool

Replaced single rundown protection with two separate allocations (rax_6[0x13] and rax_6[0x14])
Added NdisAllocateRWLock stored at rax_6[2] (previously at rbx_1[2] as NDIS handle)
Better error path cleanup using DEREF_FILTER_MODULE throughout

security_findings.entry_point: DeviceIoControl via Fast I/O dispatch on \\.\L2BRIDGE with IOCTL code 0x12a000
call_chain: FilterFastDeviceIoControl (Fast I/O dispatch — passes raw user InputBuffer pointer as arg3/rcx)
L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler(arg1=InputBuffer, arg2=InputBufferLength, ...) — dereferences *arg1 at entry WITHOUT ProbeForRead
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing ProbeForRead on user-mode pointer dereference (CWE-125: Out-of-bounds Read)
security_findings.description: The unpatched L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler receives a raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer from the Fast I/O device-control path (FilterFastDeviceIoControl). Although the IOCTL is METHOD_BUFFERED (which under normal IRP dispatch would be copied into a kernel SystemBuffer), the request is serviced by the driver's FastIoDeviceControl routine, which passes the caller's raw user-mode pointer directly with no copy. The handler immediately dereferences this pointer with 'movups xmm0, xmmword [rcx]' at 0x1c0009c6f to load 16 bytes (two 8-byte bridge-interface identifiers), BEFORE checking the InputBufferLength (cmp edx, 0x10 at 0x1c0009c85) and BEFORE any NULL check or ProbeForRead. The unpatched binary does not even import ProbeForRead or ExGetPreviousMode. The demonstrable impact is a reliable kernel BSOD (denial of service) by passing an invalid/unmapped pointer. The 16 bytes are consumed only as two lookup keys inside L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule and are never returned to the caller, so the read is blind and does not by itself disclose kernel memory. The patched version adds an ExGetPreviousMode() gate, a NULL check (!arg1), and ProbeForRead(arg1, 0x10, 4) before any dereference.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open a handle to \\.\L2BRIDGE device using CreateFileW. 2. Call DeviceIoControl with dwIoControlCode=0x12a000, which decodes as DeviceType=0x12 (FILE_DEVICE_NETWORK), Function=0x800, Method=0 (METHOD_BUFFERED), Access=2 (FILE_WRITE_ACCESS). Because the request is handled by the FastIoDeviceControl routine (which returns TRUE), the raw InputBuffer pointer is forwarded despite the METHOD_BUFFERED code. 3. Set lpInBuffer to ANY address — it is dereferenced as a raw pointer. 4. Set nInBufferSize to any value; the dereference at 0x1c0009c6f happens unconditionally before the cmp edx, 0x10 at 0x1c0009c85, so the read ALWAYS occurs regardless of nInBufferSize. For a BSOD: pass lpInBuffer pointing to unmapped memory (e.g. 0x1 or 0xDEADBEEF). Set nOutBufferSize and lpOutBuffer to 0 (the handler requires OutputBuffer==0 and OutputBufferLength==0 to reach the lookup path).
security_findings.primitive: Local kernel denial of service (reliable BSOD) from a caller that can open the device: an unmapped attacker-supplied pointer faults in movups xmm0, [rcx] at 0x1c0009c6f before any validation. The same instruction performs a 16-byte read from the attacker-supplied address, but that value is used only as two lookup keys in L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule and is never returned to the caller, so it is a blind read, not an information-disclosure primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler @ 0x1c0009c5c (UNPATCHED):

0x1c0009c5c  mov     r11, rsp
0x1c0009c5f  mov     qword [r11+0x10], rbx
0x1c0009c63  mov     qword [r11+0x18], rbp
0x1c0009c67  push    rsi
0x1c0009c68  push    rdi
0x1c0009c69  push    r14
0x1c0009c6b  sub     rsp, 0x40
0x1c0009c6f  movups  xmm0, xmmword [rcx]     ; <<< VULN: raw user ptr deref, NO probe
0x1c0009c72  xor     eax, eax
0x1c0009c74  xor     edi, edi
0x1c0009c76  xor     esi, esi
0x1c0009c78  mov     qword [r11+0x8], rax
0x1c0009c7c  xor     r14b, r14b
0x1c0009c7f  movdqu  xmmword [rsp+0x30], xmm0
0x1c0009c85  cmp     edx, 0x10               ; size check AFTER dereference
0x1c0009c88  jb      0x1c0009d92
0x1c0009c8e  test    r9d, r9d
0x1c0009c91  jne     0x1c0009d92
0x1c0009c97  test    r8, r8
0x1c0009c9a  jne     0x1c0009d92
0x1c0009ca0  mov     rcx, qword [r11-0x28]
0x1c0009ca4  call    0x1c0009bb0             ; L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule(zmm0)
0x1c0009ca9  mov     rdi, rax
0x1c0009cac  test    rax, rax
0x1c0009caf  jne     0x1c0009cbb
0x1c0009cb1  mov     ebx, 0xc0000225
0x1c0009cb6  jmp     0x1c0009e1d
0x1c0009cbb  mov     rcx, qword [rsp+0x38]
0x1c0009cc0  call    0x1c0009bb0             ; L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule(second GUID)
0x1c0009cc5  mov     rsi, rax
0x1c0009cc8  test    rax, rax
0x1c0009ccb  jne     0x1c0009cd7
0x1c0009cd7  mov     eax, dword [rax+0x84]
0x1c0009cdd  cmp     dword [rdi+0x84], eax
0x1c0009ce3  je      0x1c0009cef
...
0x1c0009e1d  mov     rbp, qword [rsp+0x70]
0x1c0009e22  mov     eax, ebx
0x1c0009e24  mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x68]
0x1c0009e29  add     rsp, 0x40
0x1c0009e2d  pop     r14
0x1c0009e2f  pop     rdi
0x1c0009e30  pop     rsi
0x1c0009e31  retn

FilterFastDeviceIoControl @ 0x1c0009b40 (UNPATCHED):
0x1c0009b40  mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx
0x1c0009b45  push    rdi
0x1c0009b46  sub     rsp, 0x20
0x1c0009b4a  xor     ecx, ecx
0x1c0009b4c  mov     ebx, r9d
0x1c0009b4f  mov     rdi, r8          ; rdi = InputBuffer (raw user ptr)
0x1c0009b52  call    qword [IoIs32bitProcess]
0x1c0009b59  nop
0x1c0009b5e  test    al, al
0x1c0009b60  je      0x1c0009b69
0x1c0009b69  cmp     dword [rsp+0x60], 0x12a000  ; check IOCTL code
0x1c0009b71  je      0x1c0009b7a
0x1c0009b7a  mov     r9d, dword [rsp+0x58]
0x1c0009b7f  mov     edx, ebx          ; edx = InputBufferLength
0x1c0009b81  mov     r8, qword [rsp+0x50]
0x1c0009b86  mov     rcx, rdi           ; rcx = InputBuffer (RAW user ptr)
0x1c0009b89  call    0x1c0009c5c        ; L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler(InputBuffer, ...)
...
0x1c0009b9f  mov     al, 0x1            ; return TRUE = Fast I/O handled

breakpoints.why: This is the entry point of the vulnerable function. At this point RCX contains the raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer that is about to be dereferenced at 0x1c0009c6f without probing. Set breakpoint here to inspect rcx before the vulnerable dereference.

The Fast I/O dispatch entry. At 0x1c0009b86 (mov rcx, rdi) the raw InputBuffer is loaded into rcx before calling L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler at 0x1c0009b89. Inspect rdi/r8 here to see the attacker-controlled pointer.

watch: RCX at 0x1c0009c6f — this is the raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer being dereferenced by movups xmm0, [rcx]. An attacker controls this value entirely.
EDX at 0x1c0009c85 — this is InputBufferLength (arg2). Note the dereference at 0x1c0009c6f occurs BEFORE this check, so even edx=0 triggers the bug.
XMM0 after 0x1c0009c6f — contains the 16 bytes read from the attacker-controlled address. These bytes are used as a GUID for L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule lookup.
key_offsets: 0x1c0009c6f — movups xmm0, xmmword [rcx]: THE VULNERABLE INSTRUCTION — unconditional dereference of raw user-mode pointer without ProbeForRead
0x1c0009c85 — cmp edx, 0x10: the InputBufferLength check that SHOULD have been before the dereference but is AFTER it
0x1c0009ca4 — call 0x1c0009bb0 (L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule): uses the unprobed data as a GUID to look up bridge interfaces
0x1c0009b89 — call 0x1c0009c5c in FilterFastDeviceIoControl: the call that passes the raw user pointer to the handler
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Open device: HANDLE h = CreateFileW(L"\\\\.\\L2BRIDGE", GENERIC_READ|GENERIC_WRITE, 0, NULL, OPEN_EXISTING, 0, NULL); 2. For BSOD: DeviceIoControl(h, 0x12a000, (LPVOID)0x1, 0x10, NULL, 0, &bytesReturned, NULL); — the pointer 0x1 is unmapped, causing a kernel access violation in the movups at 0x1c0009c6f. 3. For the blind read: set lpInBuffer to a valid kernel address. The 16-byte read result flows into L2BridgeFindandReferenceFilterModule which returns STATUS_NOT_FOUND (0xc0000225) if the keys don't match; the read value is never returned to the caller, so this observes nothing about the read bytes except through a debugger.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: For BSOD trigger: BugCheck 0x50 (PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA) with faulting address = the invalid pointer value passed as InputBuffer. The fault occurs at instruction 0x1c0009c6f (movups xmm0, xmmword [rcx]). For a valid mapped kernel address: no crash — the 16 bytes are read and used only internally, and are not returned to user mode. A debugger can observe XMM0 after 0x1c0009c6f to see the read bytes, but the caller cannot.
debugging_aids.notes: The device \Device\l2bridge is registered with no explicit security descriptor (SDDL), so it inherits default NDIS device permissions; the effective ACL should be verified on the target build. The symbolic link \DosDevices\L2BRIDGE makes it accessible as \\.\L2BRIDGE. The IOCTL 0x12a000 decodes as: DeviceType=0x12 (FILE_DEVICE_NETWORK), Function=0x800, Method=0 (METHOD_BUFFERED), Access=2 (FILE_WRITE_ACCESS). Under normal IRP dispatch a METHOD_BUFFERED IOCTL would be copied into a kernel SystemBuffer, but the driver services this request through its FastIoDeviceControl routine, which forwards the caller's raw InputBuffer pointer with no copy. The 8-byte struct layout shift visible across many functions is due to a new RWLock field added in the patched FILTER_MODULE structure (the compared field moves 0x84 -> 0x8c, the bridge pointer 0xd8 -> 0xe0, the rundown 0x98 -> 0xa0).

summary: The patch fixes a missing ProbeForRead vulnerability (CWE-125) in the L2Bridge NDIS lightweight filter driver's IOCTL handler. The unpatched L2BridgeCtlBridgeInterfaces_Handler (reachable via the Fast I/O device-control routine on \\.\L2BRIDGE with IOCTL 0x12a000, which decodes as FILE_DEVICE_NETWORK/Function 0x800/METHOD_BUFFERED but forwards the raw InputBuffer because it is serviced through Fast I/O) dereferences the raw user-mode InputBuffer pointer at 0x1c0009c6f (movups xmm0, [rcx]) BEFORE any validation or probing. The demonstrable impact is a reliable kernel denial of service (BSOD) from an unmapped attacker-supplied pointer; the 16 bytes read are used only as internal lookup keys and are never returned to the caller, so the read is blind and not a self-contained information leak. The patched version adds ExGetPreviousMode(), a NULL pointer check, and ProbeForRead(arg1, 0x10, 4). The unpatched binary does not import ProbeForRead or ExGetPreviousMode. A trigger needs only CreateFile on \\.\L2BRIDGE followed by DeviceIoControl with code 0x12a000 and an attacker-controlled InputBuffer pointer.
